title: Monsoon retreats from 70% of India: Rainfall drops across 9 states, Sri Ganganagar records 42°C; Ganga leve... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Cloudburst in J&K's Pahalgam:Sweeps away road; landslide near Uttarakhand hydropower project buries vehicles, machinery
- Copy link
Rain in India's hilly states has caused rivers to swell, triggering floods and landslides. A cloudburst in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir, led to flash flooding, damaging farmland and washing away roads.
In Uttarakhand, heavy rainfall triggered a landslide at the Lakhwar Hydroelectric Project in Vikasnagar, burying several vehicles and construction machinery under debris.
Meanwhile, the monsoon has weakened across much of the country, with rain-bearing clouds retreating from around 70% of India. Little or no rainfall is expected over the next five days in Rajasthan, Delhi-NCR, western Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat and Maharashtra.
With rainfall easing, temperatures have risen across several northern states, including Madhya Pradesh, Delhi and western Uttar Pradesh. Sriganganagar in Rajasthan recorded a maximum temperature of 42°C.
Satellite image from July 12, showing clouds disappearing from the west
Weakening weather system causes monsoon to retreat across 70% of India
The weather system responsible for sustaining the southwest monsoon has weakened, leading to a sharp decline in rainfall across much of India.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), monsoon clouds have retreated from around 70% of the country as no strong low-pressure system has developed over the Bay of Bengal since 9 July. As a result, the monsoon winds have been unable to draw in sufficient moisture.
The IMD also said the monsoon trough has shifted north of its normal position, significantly reducing cloud formation and rainfall over large parts of central, western and southern India.
At present, rainfall is largely confined to northern, eastern and north-eastern India. The IMD expects the monsoon to remain weak across most parts of the country for the next few days.

Dense fog blankets Shimla, Rishikesh and Jashpur after heavy rainfall
Heavy rainfall brought a sharp drop in temperatures across parts of northern India, with dense fog engulfing Shimla and Rishikesh on Saturday morning and evening. Visibility fell to below 50 metres, disrupting normal conditions.
In Chhattisgarh's Jashpur district, intermittent rain continued throughout Saturday, triggering thick fog in the hilly Sanna valley. Visibility dropped to less than seven metres, making travel difficult in the area.
